title: An Act respecting the Facilitating Agricultural Regulatory Modernization Act
fullText: C-273 1 45 3-4 Charles III 2025 An Act respecting the Facilitating Agricultural Regulatory Modernization Act SUMMARY This enactment amends the Feeds Act, the Fertilizers Act, the Seeds Act, the Pest Control Products Act and the Food and Drugs Act to introduce trusted-jurisdiction reliance, provisional and final approval or registration regimes for agricultural inputs, and to expand foreign-regulatory-authority deeming orders to include veterinary drugs. His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows: Short Title 1 This Act may be cited as the Facilitating Agricultural Regulatory Modernization Act. Feeds Act R.S., c. F-9 2 Section 2 of the Feeds Act is amended by adding the following definition in alphabetical order: trusted jurisdiction means a jurisdiction prescribed by regulation as having regulatory standards for feeds that the Minister considers to provide a level of protection of human and animal health and the environment that is comparable to the level of protection provided under this Act. 3 Section 3 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1): Trusted jurisdiction (1.1) Despite paragraphs (1)(a) and (b), a feed may be manufactured, sold or imported into Canada if it has been approved or registered by the regulatory authority of a trusted jurisdiction and the prescribed conditions are satisfied. 4 The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5.2: Provisional approval or registration 5.21 (1) On application, the Minister may grant a provisional approval or registration of a feed for a period of not more than two years if the Minister is satisfied that the prescribed criteria are met and that granting the provisional approval or registration is unlikely to result in harm to human or animal health or the environment. Conditions (2) The Minister may make a provisional approval or registration subject to the prescribed conditions and to any additional conditions the Minister considers appropriate. Final approval or registration 5.22 The Minister shall, before the end of the period referred to in subsection 5.21(1), either grant a final approval or registration of the feed, refuse the application or extend the provisional approval or registration for a further period not exceeding one year. 5 Subsection 5(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b.1): (b.2) respecting provisional and final approvals and registrations of feeds, including the criteria, terms and conditions to be imposed under sections 5.21 and 5.22; (b.3) prescribing trusted jurisdictions and the conditions under which a feed approved or registered by a trusted jurisdiction may be manufactured, sold or imported into Canada; Fertilizers Act R.S., c. F-10 6 Section 2 of the Fertilizers Act is amended by adding the following definition in alphabetical order: trusted jurisdiction means a jurisdiction prescribed by regulation as having regulatory standards for fertilizers and supplements that the Minister considers to provide a level of protection of human and animal health and the environment that is comparable to the level of protection provided under this Act. 7 Section 3 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1): Trusted jurisdiction (1.1) Despite paragraphs (1)(a) and (b), a fertilizer or supplement may be manufactured, sold or imported into Canada if it has been approved or registered by the regulatory authority of a trusted jurisdiction and the prescribed conditions are satisfied. 8 The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5.2: Provisional approval or registration 5.21 (1) On application, the Minister may grant a provisional approval or registration of a fertilizer or supplement for a period of not more than two years if the Minister is satisfied that the prescribed criteria are met and that granting the provisional approval or registration is unlikely to result in harm to human or animal health or the environment. Final approval or registration 5.22 The Minister shall, before the end of the period referred to in subsection 5.21(1), either grant a final approval or registration of the fertilizer or supplement, refuse the application or extend the provisional approval or registration for a further period not exceeding one year. 9 Subsection 5(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following: (b.2) respecting provisional and final approvals and registrations of fertilizers and supplements; (b.3) prescribing trusted jurisdictions and the conditions under which a fertilizer or supplement approved or registered by a trusted jurisdiction may be manufactured, sold or imported into Canada; Seeds Act R.S., c. S-8 10 Section 2 of the Seeds Act is amended by adding the following definition in alphabetical order: trusted jurisdiction means a jurisdiction prescribed by regulation as having regulatory standards for seed varieties that the Minister considers to provide a level of protection comparable to the level of protection provided under this Act. 11 The Act is amended by adding the following after section 4: Provisional variety registration 4.1 (1) On application, the Minister may grant a provisional registration of a seed variety for a period of not more than two years if the Minister is satisfied that the prescribed criteria are met. Final variety registration 4.2 The Minister shall, before the end of the period referred to in subsection 4.1(1), either grant a final registration of the variety, refuse the application or extend the provisional registration for a further period not exceeding one year. 12 The Act is amended by adding the following after section 4.2: Trusted jurisdiction reliance 4.3 The Minister may register a seed variety on the basis of a registration granted by the regulatory authority of a trusted jurisdiction if the prescribed conditions are satisfied. 13 Section 4 of the Act is amended by adding the following: (k) respecting provisional and final variety registrations under sections 4.1 and 4.2; (l) prescribing trusted jurisdictions and the conditions under which a seed variety registered by a trusted jurisdiction may be registered under section 4.3; Pest Control Products Act S.C. 2002, c. 28 14 Section 2 of the Pest Control Products Act is amended by adding the following definition: trusted jurisdiction means a jurisdiction prescribed by regulation as having regulatory standards for pest control products that the Minister considers to provide a level of protection of health and the environment that is comparable to the level of protection provided under this Act. 15 The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7: Provisional registration 7.1 (1) On application, the Minister may grant a provisional registration of a pest control product for a period of not more than two years if the Minister is satisfied that the prescribed criteria are met and that the value of the product and its health and environmental risks are acceptable. Final registration 7.2 The Minister shall, before the end of the period referred to in subsection 7.1(1), either grant a final registration of the pest control product, refuse the application or extend the provisional registration for a further period not exceeding one year. 16 The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7.2: Trusted jurisdiction reliance 7.3 The Minister may register a pest control product on the basis of a registration granted by the regulatory authority of a trusted jurisdiction if the prescribed conditions are satisfied. 17 Section 67(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following: (z.6) respecting provisional and final registrations under sections 7.1 and 7.2; (z.7) prescribing trusted jurisdictions and the conditions under which a pest control product registered by a trusted jurisdiction may be registered under section 7.3; 18 The Act is amended by adding the following after section 80: Coordination with trusted jurisdictions 80.1 The Minister may enter into an arrangement with the regulatory authority of a trusted jurisdiction respecting the exchange of information, joint reviews and the alignment of decisions in respect of pest control products. Food and Drugs Act R.S., c. F-27 19 Subsection 30.06(1) of the Food and Drugs Act is replaced by the following: 30.06 (1) Subject to the regulations, the Minister may, by order, designate a foreign regulatory authority for the purposes of this section in respect of a therapeutic product, a veterinary drug or a class of either of them, and may, in that order, deem any decision made by that foreign regulatory authority in respect of the therapeutic product, the veterinary drug or the class to be a decision made under this Act. Coming into Force Coming into force 20 This Act comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

sections.text: 1 This Act may be cited as the Facilitating Agricultural Regulatory Modernization Act.

2 Section 2 of the Feeds Act is amended by adding the following definition in alphabetical order: trusted jurisdiction means a jurisdiction prescribed by regulation as having regulatory standards for feeds that the Minister considers to provide a level of protection of human and animal health and the environment that is comparable to the level of protection provided under this Act.

3 Section 3 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1): Trusted jurisdiction (1.1) Despite paragraphs (1)(a) and (b), a feed may be manufactured, sold or imported into Canada if it has been approved or registered by the regulatory authority of a trusted jurisdiction and the prescribed conditions are satisfied.

sections.marginalNote: Provisional and final approval or registration
sections.text: 4 The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5.2: Provisional approval or registration 5.21 (1) On application, the Minister may grant a provisional approval or registration of a feed for a period of not more than two years if the Minister is satisfied that the prescribed criteria are met and that granting the provisional approval or registration is unlikely to result in harm to human or animal health or the environment. Conditions (2) The Minister may make a provisional approval or registration subject to the prescribed conditions and to any additional conditions the Minister considers appropriate. Final approval or registration 5.22 The Minister shall, before the end of the period referred to in subsection 5.21(1), either grant a final approval or registration of the feed, refuse the application or extend the provisional approval or registration for a further period not exceeding one year.

5 Subsection 5(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b.1): (b.2) respecting provisional and final approvals and registrations of feeds, including the criteria, terms and conditions to be imposed under sections 5.21 and 5.22; (b.3) prescribing trusted jurisdictions and the conditions under which a feed approved or registered by a trusted jurisdiction may be manufactured, sold or imported into Canada;

6 Section 2 of the Fertilizers Act is amended by adding the following definition in alphabetical order: trusted jurisdiction means a jurisdiction prescribed by regulation as having regulatory standards for fertilizers and supplements that the Minister considers to provide a level of protection of human and animal health and the environment that is comparable to the level of protection provided under this Act.

7 Section 3 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1): Trusted jurisdiction (1.1) Despite paragraphs (1)(a) and (b), a fertilizer or supplement may be manufactured, sold or imported into Canada if it has been approved or registered by the regulatory authority of a trusted jurisdiction and the prescribed conditions are satisfied.

sections.marginalNote: Provisional and final approval or registration
sections.text: 8 The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5.2: Provisional approval or registration 5.21 (1) On application, the Minister may grant a provisional approval or registration of a fertilizer or supplement for a period of not more than two years if the Minister is satisfied that the prescribed criteria are met and that granting the provisional approval or registration is unlikely to result in harm to human or animal health or the environment. Final approval or registration 5.22 The Minister shall, before the end of the period referred to in subsection 5.21(1), either grant a final approval or registration of the fertilizer or supplement, refuse the application or extend the provisional approval or registration for a further period not exceeding one year.

9 Subsection 5(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following: (b.2) respecting provisional and final approvals and registrations of fertilizers and supplements; (b.3) prescribing trusted jurisdictions and the conditions under which a fertilizer or supplement approved or registered by a trusted jurisdiction may be manufactured, sold or imported into Canada;

10 Section 2 of the Seeds Act is amended by adding the following definition in alphabetical order: trusted jurisdiction means a jurisdiction prescribed by regulation as having regulatory standards for seed varieties that the Minister considers to provide a level of protection comparable to the level of protection provided under this Act.

11 The Act is amended by adding the following after section 4: Provisional variety registration 4.1 (1) On application, the Minister may grant a provisional registration of a seed variety for a period of not more than two years if the Minister is satisfied that the prescribed criteria are met. Final variety registration 4.2 The Minister shall, before the end of the period referred to in subsection 4.1(1), either grant a final registration of the variety, refuse the application or extend the provisional registration for a further period not exceeding one year.

12 The Act is amended by adding the following after section 4.2: Trusted jurisdiction reliance 4.3 The Minister may register a seed variety on the basis of a registration granted by the regulatory authority of a trusted jurisdiction if the prescribed conditions are satisfied.

13 Section 4 of the Act is amended by adding the following: (k) respecting provisional and final variety registrations under sections 4.1 and 4.2; (l) prescribing trusted jurisdictions and the conditions under which a seed variety registered by a trusted jurisdiction may be registered under section 4.3;

14 Section 2 of the Pest Control Products Act is amended by adding the following definition: trusted jurisdiction means a jurisdiction prescribed by regulation as having regulatory standards for pest control products that the Minister considers to provide a level of protection of health and the environment that is comparable to the level of protection provided under this Act.

15 The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7: Provisional registration 7.1 (1) On application, the Minister may grant a provisional registration of a pest control product for a period of not more than two years if the Minister is satisfied that the prescribed criteria are met and that the value of the product and its health and environmental risks are acceptable. Final registration 7.2 The Minister shall, before the end of the period referred to in subsection 7.1(1), either grant a final registration of the pest control product, refuse the application or extend the provisional registration for a further period not exceeding one year.

16 The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7.2: Trusted jurisdiction reliance 7.3 The Minister may register a pest control product on the basis of a registration granted by the regulatory authority of a trusted jurisdiction if the prescribed conditions are satisfied.

17 Section 67(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following: (z.6) respecting provisional and final registrations under sections 7.1 and 7.2; (z.7) prescribing trusted jurisdictions and the conditions under which a pest control product registered by a trusted jurisdiction may be registered under section 7.3;

18 The Act is amended by adding the following after section 80: Coordination with trusted jurisdictions 80.1 The Minister may enter into an arrangement with the regulatory authority of a trusted jurisdiction respecting the exchange of information, joint reviews and the alignment of decisions in respect of pest control products.

19 Subsection 30.06(1) of the Food and Drugs Act is replaced by the following: 30.06 (1) Subject to the regulations, the Minister may, by order, designate a foreign regulatory authority for the purposes of this section in respect of a therapeutic product, a veterinary drug or a class of either of them, and may, in that order, deem any decision made by that foreign regulatory authority in respect of the therapeutic product, the veterinary drug or the class to be a decision made under this Act.
20 This Act comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
